description: Emerging semantic signals under watch for Vortik. These are not canonical anchors and should not be treated as protocol-stable registry primitives yet.

signals.name: Block Access List
signals.summary: Execution-layer coordination object under watch as Ethereum decomposes execution into narrower protocol-facing objects such as access lists, sidecars, state-healing inputs, and execution-resource descriptions.
why_it_matters: Signals movement toward concrete execution-layer terminology rather than broad market abstractions.
May pressure broad labels such as execution market and blockspace market.
Could become a durable execution-facing semantic surface if convergence continues.

signals.name: Payload Timeliness Committee
signals.summary: Protocol-facing committee concept associated with ePBS timing, payload reveal accountability, and builder/proposer coordination.
why_it_matters: Directly relates to ePBS coordination and payload reveal timing.
Could become an important sub-surface around builder accountability.
Strengthens the need to track roles below the main ePBS primitive.

signals.name: Inclusion List Committee
signals.summary: Validator committee role associated with constructing or enforcing inclusion lists in FOCIL-like designs.
why_it_matters: Introduces a concrete role inside inclusion-list enforcement.
Separates inclusion enforcement responsibility from a single proposer.
Could become more important if FOCIL terminology continues to stabilize.

signals.name: Partial Block Auction
signals.summary: Auction mechanism applied to part of a block, especially where inclusion-list-constrained space coexists with competitive allocation of remaining block capacity.
why_it_matters: Connects inclusion constraints with residual block construction markets.
May become relevant if hybrid constrained/auctioned block construction models stabilize.
Naming remains unstable and should not be treated as canonical yet.

signals.name: Slot Auction
signals.summary: Time-based rights allocation surface where rights to propose, influence, or economically control a future slot may be auctioned ahead of time.
why_it_matters: May intersect with execution tickets or proposer-rights markets.
Captures a reusable coordination pattern around future slot control.
Terminology is ambiguous and should remain watchlist-only.

signals.name: Builder Fallback
signals.summary: Fallback path where proposers or protocol participants revert to alternative block-building behavior if external builders fail, censor, or do not reveal payloads.
why_it_matters: Relevant to PBS and ePBS robustness.
Connects builder failure, liveness, and censorship resistance.
Naming remains descriptive rather than canonical.

signals.name: Based Sequencing
signals.summary: Rollup sequencing model where L2 ordering is anchored to L1 proposers rather than independent external sequencers.
why_it_matters: Important to rollup sequencing and Ethereum-adjacent ordering design.
May influence preconfirmation, sequencing, and proposer-linked coordination.
Relevant but external to the current L1 core registry.

signals.name: LUCID
signals.summary: Encrypted-orderflow signal connecting inclusion guarantees, commit-before-reveal flow, and MEV-sensitive transaction protection.
why_it_matters: May strengthen the semantic importance of inclusion lists as a public-facing primitive.
May increase relevance of commitment-oriented vocabulary.
May pressure order-flow auction language if encrypted mempool terminology wins.

signals.name: Execution Tickets
signals.summary: Forward-looking execution coordination term under watch. Not yet treated as a canonical Vortik anchor.
why_it_matters: Could become relevant if future execution coordination work converges around the term.
Still too early for canonical treatment.
Should remain watchlist-only until stronger protocol or implementation references stabilize.

signals.name: Single Slot Finality
signals.summary: Finality roadmap concept under watch as SSF continues to pressure broader fast-finality language.
why_it_matters: May pressure fastfinality.eth if SSF becomes the dominant exact term.
Relevant to long-term Ethereum roadmap semantics.
Should remain watchlist-only unless the registry later adds a more exact SSF-aligned anchor.

signals.name: Key Provider
signals.summary: Potential future coordination role relevant to encrypted mempool or threshold decryption systems if protected order flow becomes more protocol-shaped.
why_it_matters: Could emerge as a meaningful role if encrypted transaction flow becomes more formalized.
Represents a possible semantic surface around protected order flow.
Still too early for registry-level treatment.

ignored_terms.name: Ranking Fee
ignored_terms.reason: Too narrow and design-specific. Better treated as internal mechanism vocabulary rather than a reusable semantic anchor.

ignored_terms.name: Inclusion Constraint
ignored_terms.reason: Conceptually valid but too generic. Better captured through inclusionlist and FOCIL.

ignored_terms.name: Escrowed Bids
ignored_terms.reason: Broad financial mechanism phrase with low Ethereum-specific naming distinctiveness.
